questions.text: Which of the following is the genetic material in most organisms?
questions.explanation: Deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) is the primary genetic material, though some viruses use RNA.

questions.text: A nucleotide consists of:
questions.explanation: A nitrogenous base is linked to a pentose sugar and a phosphate group.

questions.text: Chargaff's rule states that in a double-stranded DNA:
questions.explanation: The ratio between Adenine and Thymine, and Guanine and Cytosine are constant and equal to one (Purines = Pyrimidines).

questions.text: The double helix model of DNA was proposed by:
questions.explanation: James Watson and Francis Crick proposed the model in 1953 based on X-ray diffraction data.

questions.text: DNA strands are antiparallel because of:
questions.explanation: One strand has 5'→3' polarity and the other has 3'→5' polarity.

questions.text: The pitch of the DNA helix is:
questions.explanation: There are roughly 10 base pairs in each turn (pitch) of 3.4 nm.

questions.text: Histones are rich in which basic amino acids?
questions.explanation: Histones are positively charged proteins that help in packaging negatively charged DNA.

questions.text: The 'beads-on-string' structure seen under electron microscope is:
questions.explanation: Nucleosomes are the repeating units of chromatin consisting of DNA wrapped around a histone octamer.

questions.text: The 'transforming principle' was first discovered by ________ using Streptococcus pneumoniae.
questions.explanation: Frederick Griffith (1928) observed that heat-killed S strain bacteria could transform live R strain into virulent S strain.

questions.text: Hershey and Chase used which radioactive isotopes to prove DNA is the genetic material?
questions.explanation: They labeled DNA with radioactive phosphorus (³²P) and proteins with radioactive sulfur (³⁵S).

questions.text: DNA is more stable than RNA because of:
options: Presence of Thymine instead of Uracil
questions.explanation: The chemical modifications and structure of DNA make it less reactive and more suitable for storage of genetic info.

questions.text: The semiconservative nature of DNA replication was experimentally proven by:
questions.explanation: Meselson and Stahl used E. coli and heavy nitrogen (¹⁵N); Taylor used Vicia faba and tritiated thymidine.

questions.text: The enzyme that catalyzes the polymerization of deoxynucleotides is:
questions.explanation: DNA-dependent DNA polymerase adds nucleotides in the 5'→3' direction.

questions.text: Small fragments of DNA formed on the lagging strand are called:
questions.explanation: Because DNA polymerase only works in the 5'→3' direction, one strand is synthesized discontinuously.

questions.text: The process of copying genetic information from DNA to RNA is:
questions.explanation: Transcription occurs in the nucleus (eukaryotes) and is mediated by RNA polymerase.

questions.text: In a transcription unit, the strand that has the same sequence as RNA (except T instead of U) is the:
questions.explanation: The coding strand (5'→3') is used as the reference point for defining the promoter and terminator.

questions.text: The non-coding sequences in eukaryotic genes are called:
questions.explanation: Introns are removed during RNA splicing to form functional mRNA.

questions.text: The process of removing introns and joining exons is called:
questions.explanation: Splicing is a key post-transcriptional modification in eukaryotes.

questions.text: Which of the following is the 'start codon'?
questions.explanation: AUG codes for Methionine and acts as the initiation signal for translation.

questions.text: The relationship between the sequence of nucleotides on mRNA and the sequence of amino acids is the:
questions.explanation: The genetic code is triplet, universal, and non-overlapping.

questions.text: The adapter molecule that reads the code and brings amino acids is:
questions.explanation: Transfer RNA (tRNA) has an anticodon loop and an amino acid acceptor end.

questions.text: Translation refers to the process of:
questions.explanation: Amino acids are polymerized into a polypeptide chain based on the mRNA sequence.

questions.text: The Lac operon is switched 'ON' when ________ is present.
questions.explanation: Lactose acts as an inducer by binding to the repressor protein.

questions.text: Who developed the 'DNA Fingerprinting' technique?
questions.explanation: Alec Jeffreys used satellite DNA (VNTRs) to identify individuals based on genetic variation.

questions.text: Human Genome Project was launched in the year:
questions.explanation: The 13-year mega project was completed in 2003.

questions.text: The human genome contains approximately how many base pairs?
questions.explanation: The haploid human genome has roughly 3.3 billion base pairs.

questions.text: VNTR stands for:
questions.explanation: VNTRs belong to a class of satellite DNA referred to as mini-satellites.

questions.text: The core enzyme of RNA polymerase in bacteria needs which factor to initiate transcription?
questions.explanation: Sigma factor helps the polymerase recognize the promoter; Rho factor helps in termination.

questions.text: Degeneracy of the genetic code means:
options: One codon codes for many amino acids
One amino acid is coded by more than one codon
questions.explanation: Because there are 61 codons for 20 amino acids, some amino acids have multiple synonymous codons.

questions.text: Central Dogma of molecular biology was proposed by:
questions.explanation: Crick stated that information flows from DNA → RNA → Protein.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a stop codon?
questions.explanation: UGG codes for Tryptophan. UAA, UAG, and UGA are termination codons.

questions.text: The nitrogenous base absent in RNA is:
questions.explanation: In RNA, Uracil is present in place of Thymine.

questions.text: In a DNA strand, nucleotides are linked by:
questions.explanation: A 3'-5' phosphodiester bond links the sugar of one nucleotide to the phosphate of the next.

questions.text: Euchromatin is:
questions.explanation: Euchromatin stains light and is transcriptionally active compared to heterochromatin.

questions.text: DNA replication occurs in which phase of the cell cycle?
questions.explanation: The S (Synthesis) phase is when DNA content of the cell is doubled.

questions.text: RNA polymerase III in eukaryotes transcribes:
questions.explanation: Eukaryotes have three RNA polymerases with specific targets (Pol I for rRNA, Pol II for hnRNA).

questions.text: A cistron is:
options: A segment of DNA coding for a polypeptide
questions.explanation: Eukaryotic structural genes are usually monocistronic, while bacteria are polycistronic.

questions.text: The sequence of amino acids in a protein is determined by the sequence of:
questions.explanation: mRNA carries the genetic message from DNA to the ribosome.

questions.text: How many codons code for amino acids?
questions.explanation: Of the 64 possible codons, 3 are stop codons and do not code for any amino acid.

questions.text: SNP stands for:
questions.explanation: These are single base DNA differences observed in the human population.
